frameworks.name: partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP)
frameworks.one_line: action selection over posterior belief states under sensory uncertainty

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference in perception
frameworks.one_line: probabilistic perceptual representations extended to drive action selection

frameworks.name: temporal difference (TD) reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value learning over belief states to maximize expected reward

frameworks.name: cortico-basal-ganglia decision architecture
frameworks.one_line: neocortex computes belief; basal ganglia handle value and action selection

core_question: How are probabilistic perceptual representations used by the brain to select actions under uncertainty?
key_assumptions: brain performs Bayesian inference during perception
actions depend on the full posterior belief state, not a point estimate
expected reward is maximized via TD learning
neocortex represents beliefs while basal ganglia evaluate and select actions
manipulations: application to random dots motion discrimination task
tasks with versus without a decision deadline
trading off information gathering against overt action
key_findings: model neurons coding belief resemble primate LIP responses on motion discrimination
an appropriate threshold for switching from sampling to action emerges from reward maximization
with a deadline, the learned decision strategy yields a collapsing threshold over elapsed time
interpretations: POMDP + TD provides a unified account of perceptual decision making with information gathering and overt actions
cortex-basal ganglia division of labor implements belief-based action selection
